summary: Implementation-independent command-package view for generated Python and TypeScript CLI package targets.

generation_policy.authority_rule: Command/interface truth belongs in contracts and this package IR; generated target files are derived outputs.
generation_policy.runtime_boundary: Runtime primitives and live workspace inspection stay hand-owned only as explicit transitional debt; full Python generated CLI completion requires executable behavior to be generated from implementation-independent contracts or IR into generated/python outputs, with only separately inventoried package-unique primitive implementations allowed.
generation_policy.ordinary_development_environment: Python development remains sufficient for ordinary repo work.
generation_policy.test_environment: Generated non-Python package tests run in Docker-selected proof lanes.
generation_policy.custom_codegen_boundary: Custom generation code should stay small: validated IR loading, target rendering, freshness checks, and no-direct-edit sentinels.
generation_policy.shell_adapter_policy: Issue #909 evaluation selects Bash as the first additional generated command transport candidate and keeps it deferred. Promotion may start only with parser/help projection from canonical command artifacts, then black-box conformance for runtime handoff; PowerShell remains deferred until the Bash boundary and proof route are proven. Shell-family targets are downstream projections for thin wrappers, help, argument validation, completion metadata, and runtime handoff only; they must not own runtime primitive behavior.
generation_policy.direct_cli_edit_policy: Interface shape, generated package metadata, adapter wiring, and shipped CLI behavior must move through contracts, IR, generators, and regenerated outputs instead of package-local executable cli.py files; direct cli.py edits or other direct edits to package-local executable Python are transitional debt unless they are explicitly classified package-unique primitive implementations outside the generated CLI behavior surface.

extraction_readiness.rule: Reusable command-generation source must not import Agentic Workspace product modules. Product-specific literals in command-generation source require explicit accepted-coupling inventory with owner, reason, and migration path.

accepted_couplings.reason: The package-owned IR loader accepts the historical Agentic Workspace schema identity and canonicalizes it to command-generation/command-package-ir/v1 before validation and rendering.
accepted_couplings.migration_path: Keep host-side proof asserting the canonical command-generation schema after loading; remove this coupling once checked-in AW command-package IR no longer uses the legacy schema identity.

generated_package_maturity.routing_rule: Weak agents may use only generated targets whose maturity level explicitly allows weak-agent routing; proof fixtures and parser/help proofs are inspection artifacts, not usable CLI surfaces.

levels.summary: Generated metadata proves projection from the command-package IR but does not provide a runnable CLI entrypoint.
promotion_requires: target package exposes generated parser/help or command invocation glue
generated metadata remains fresh from command_package_ir.json
no-direct-edit checks cover generated files

levels.summary: Generated target can render command shape or help, but runtime command behavior is not yet callable.
promotion_requires: runtime binding model is declared outside universal command truth
read-only command invocation has a selected conformance contract
Docker-selected proof can execute the target package

levels.summary: Generated target can be invoked for a selected read-only command and returns observable command output.
promotion_requires: black-box conformance covers invocation, exit code, output shape, and one failure path
failure output distinguishes generation drift from adapter and runtime failures
ordinary Python development remains free of Node or Docker requirements

levels.summary: Generated target delegates to hand-owned runtime primitives for read-only behavior without reimplementing live inspection.
promotion_requires: runtime delegation envelope and error mapping are contract-backed
selected conformance contract passes against canonical process behavior
runtime primitive ownership remains explicit

levels.summary: Generated target is safe to route to weaker agents for supported read-only commands.
promotion_requires: help output identifies supported generated commands and the weak-agent routing status
unsupported command errors give a compact recovery path instead of leaving agents to infer next steps
native TypeScript runtime failures are classified with recovery guidance
black-box conformance covers normal read-only behavior, unsupported command recovery, and Python-free native execution
generated-package proof advertises the target as weak-agent-safe only after these checks pass

levels.summary: Generated target can invoke mutation-capable behavior through guarded runtime primitives.
promotion_requires: dry-run and apply behavior are separately covered
preflight and destructive-refusal guards have black-box conformance
human review requirements are surfaced before unsafe mutation

levels.summary: Target is intentionally not generated yet.
promotion_requires: a prior maturity level is proven for Python and TypeScript read-only targets
target-specific projection requirements are defined
ordinary development remains independent of target-specific tooling

python_cli_completion.finish_line: Python CLI completion means parser, dispatch, command/interface behavior, adapter metadata, conformance expectations, product-specific runtime binding, and generic deterministic file/data behavior are generated from implementation-independent contracts or IR into generated/python outputs and executed by codegen-owned primitive executors; the command-generation package owns only generic generation, rendering, primitive execution, schema, freshness, and conformance machinery.
allowed_hand_owned_cli_responsibilities: package-specific runtime primitive implementation that cannot yet be represented by codegen-owned primitives
thin operation handler adapters that call runtime primitives until their behavior is generated
must_move_behind_contracts_or_generation: generic file, JSON, TOML, Markdown, schema, template, managed payload, and JSON/text emission behavior
product-specific executable runtime modules currently living in shipped module source or command-generation source
generated command modules that import package-owned runtime helpers directly
weak-agent routing and maturity claims for generated command surfaces
proof_requirements: generated-package proof distinguishes adapter maturity from full Python generated CLI completion

static proof rejects treating weak-agent-safe-adapter as full Python generated CLI completion while generated Python package main functions delegate generated-command parser or dispatch behavior to runtime main
future package command/interface work changes contracts, IR, generators, and regenerated outputs instead of package-local executable cli.py files
runtime primitive code remains module-owned only when it performs live behavior, mutation orchestration, or package-specific judgment that is explicitly inventoried as not yet portable

generic deterministic operations over files, JSON, TOML, Markdown, schemas, templates, managed payloads, and emitted JSON/text are codegen-owned primitive executor behavior before full completion is claimed

product-specific executable runtime behavior lives under generated/python target output and is rendered by command-generation before full completion is claimed; moving it from modules into command-generation source is not sufficient
root console smoke proof executes at least one generated root command through the installed console path, not only through generated parser/help imports

Full completion proof must distinguish generated-output placement from generated ownership; product-specific runtime files under generated/python must be produced by command-generation or explicitly inventoried as transitional generated-output debt.
Full completion proof must distinguish portable codegen primitive execution from domain runtime primitives wrapped in IR.

Full completion proof must fail while package-domain runtime source or generated runtime facades are accepted only at whole-file granularity; accepted package-domain runtime boundaries must be exact source-symbol entries with non-generic audit evidence and mechanical facade-call verification.
The compact Python completion blocker report must answer why current_state cannot honestly move to full-generated-cli-complete.

satisfied_by.evidence: Generated Python command parsers are rendered from command package IR and handwritten parser command additions are rejected.
Generated entrypoint dispatch order routes generated command handlers before compatibility fallbacks.
CLI option/help/interface behavior is backed by command package contracts and generated package checks.
Future command/interface changes must pass proof selection gating before generated completion can remain satisfied.
Python generated command behavior is covered by black-box conformance fixtures.
Python generated commands have Docker conformance coverage for packaged execution.
Runtime handlers remain explicit thin domain primitive boundaries, not hidden parser or dispatch ownership.
Representative operations are consumed through operation IR and primitive executor paths.
The operation execution inventory covers generated Python operations and their runtime boundary classifications.
The installed root console path executes a generated command through primitive IR.
Product-specific generated output ownership and exact runtime source-symbol boundaries are mechanically checked.
scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check proves generated Python CLI, runtime facade, and operation executor files are rendered from command-package IR.

python_runtime_projection_inventory.json accepted_runtime_boundaries entries cover every generated runtime facade and generated operation function-call package-domain source symbol at source-symbol granularity.
scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-completion-blockers --format json reports completion_claim_allowed=true with no blockers.

non_python_runtime_binding.selected_model: generated parser, validation, and native TypeScript/Node command execution
non_python_runtime_binding.scope: Generated TypeScript adapters execute declared generated commands through Node/TypeScript runtime code without shelling out to Python for ordinary command execution.
runtime_owns: Node filesystem and process APIs used by generated TypeScript primitives
contract-backed payload projection for generated TypeScript commands
canonical JSON or text output behavior emitted by generated TypeScript runtime helpers
error_mapping: Generated parser and validation failures are adapter failures.
Native TypeScript operation failures are TypeScript native runtime failures.
Non-zero exits emitted by native TypeScript execution are runtime failures unless the conformance case expects the error.
Generation freshness and no-direct-edit failures remain generation-layer failures before runtime execution starts.

python_runtime_binding.default_runtime_command: python -c "import sys; from agentic_workspace.cli import main; raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1:]))"
